tool_name: CBAM Financial Exposure Calculator (EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism)
category: Environmental Engineering & Corporate Finance / GHG Compliance

inputs.name: Scope 1 — Fuel Consumption (all fuels combined)
inputs.hint: Natural gas: multiply m³ × 0.0377 GJ/m³ | LPG: × 0.0474 | Coal: × 0.0264 per kg

inputs.name: Scope 1 — Weighted Fuel Emission Factor
inputs.hint: Natural gas: 0.0561 | Coal: 0.0963 | LPG: 0.0630 | Fuel oil: 0.0779 — IPCC AR6

inputs.name: Scope 1 — Direct Process Emissions (non-combustion)
inputs.hint: Steel: BF/BOF CO₂ from ore reduction | Cement: calcination CO₂ | Fertiliser: N₂O

inputs.name: EU Sector Free Allocation Benchmark
inputs.hint: Steel (hot-rolled): 1.328 | Cement clinker: 0.766 | Aluminium: 1.514 | Ammonia: 1.600 — EU Reg. 2021/447

inputs.name: Carbon Tax / ETS Already Paid in Origin Country
inputs.hint: Turkey: no carbon tax (0) | UK ETS: market price | Other: national carbon price

inputs.name: CBAM Phasing Factor (transitional)
inputs.hint: Full liability from 2026: 1.0 | CBAM fully operational after 2034 free allocation phase-out

formulas: Scope1_Combustion_tCO2e = scope1_fuel_consumption_gj * scope1_fuel_emission_factor   // [tCO₂e/year] Scope 1 combustion emissions — GHG Protocol
Total_GHG_tCO2e = Scope1_Combustion_tCO2e + scope1_process_emissions_tco2e + Scope2_tCO2e   // [tCO₂e/year] Total Scope 1+2
Specific_Embedded_tCO2_per_tonne = Total_GHG_tCO2e / annual_production_tonnes   // [tCO₂e/t product] Carbon intensity
Excess_Emissions_rate = MAX(0, Specific_Embedded_tCO2_per_tonne - eu_sector_benchmark_tco2_per_tonne)   // [tCO₂e/t] Excess above EU benchmark
Total_Excess_tCO2e = Excess_Emissions_rate * eu_export_volume_tonnes   // [tCO₂e/year] Total CBAM-liable excess
Gross_CBAM_Liability_EUR = Total_Excess_tCO2e * eu_ets_price_eur_tco2   // [EUR/year] Gross CBAM certificate cost
Local_Carbon_Already_Paid_EUR = Total_Excess_tCO2e * local_carbon_tax_eur_tco2   // [EUR/year] Deductible local carbon payments
Net_CBAM_Cost_EUR = MAX(0, (Gross_CBAM_Liability_EUR - Local_Carbon_Already_Paid_EUR) * cbam_phase_factor)   // [EUR/year] Net CBAM financial exposure
CBAM_Per_Tonne_Exported = eu_export_volume_tonnes > 0 ? Net_CBAM_Cost_EUR / eu_export_volume_tonnes : 0   // [EUR/tonne] CBAM surcharge per exported tonne
Carbon_Intensity_vs_Benchmark_Pct = (Specific_Embedded_tCO2_per_tonne / eu_sector_benchmark_tco2_per_tonne) * 100   // [%] Performance vs EU benchmark (100% = at benchmark)

export_exceeds_production.error_msg: EU export volume cannot exceed total production volume. Data input error.

production_positive.error_msg: Annual production volume must be positive — division by zero in carbon intensity calculation.

benchmark_positive.error_msg: EU sector benchmark must be positive. Check EU Regulation 2021/447 for your product category benchmark value.

smart_warnings.message: CBAM EXPOSURE CONFIRMED: Your carbon intensity exceeds the EU sector benchmark. CBAM certificates must be purchased for every tonne exported to the EU above this threshold. This is a legal obligation from 2026 onwards. Decarbonisation investment or EU market exit must be evaluated.

MATERIAL FINANCIAL EXPOSURE: Annual CBAM liability exceeds €100,000. This represents a significant margin erosion risk for EU-destined exports. Quantify in CAPEX planning for decarbonisation. EU customers will increasingly demand lower-carbon supply chains.

smart_warnings.source: EU Green Deal Supply Chain Requirements
smart_warnings.message: EXTREME CARBON INTENSITY: Your product emits 50%+ more CO₂ than the EU benchmark. At current EU ETS trajectory (projected €100+/t by 2030), export competitiveness will be critically impaired. Immediate industrial decarbonisation roadmap required.

High-carbon electricity grid (>0.5 kgCO₂/kWh): Scope 2 emissions are a major CBAM driver. Corporate PPA (Power Purchase Agreement) with renewable energy can significantly reduce embedded carbon and improve CBAM position. Model the impact using market-based Scope 2 method.

No local carbon tax offset applicable. Countries with carbon pricing (UK, Switzerland, Korea) can deduct equivalent carbon costs from CBAM liability. Monitoring Turkey's carbon market development is recommended.

More than 50% of production exported to EU: High CBAM concentration risk. Any regulatory tightening of EU benchmarks or ETS price spike will have disproportionate financial impact. Diversify export markets or accelerate low-carbon transition.
